architecture_fit.note: Jacobian ∂O/∂V is a valid attribution that correctly recovers the true pathway, but for O=A@V it tautologically equals A (still computed via softmax(QK^T) internally), so residual circularity remains.

baseline_comparison.note: Two strawmen under identical conditions — framework's uniform baseline plus an own no_softmax (relu(QK^T) row-normalised) — both visibly fail (KL 10–19 / 0.002) where the method wins.

faithfulness.note: Real causal ablation implemented: removing the top-attributed key changes output more than a random key in every condition (e.g. uniform 0.648 vs 0.109), though gaps are weak in the peaked regimes.

operating_range.note: Full qk_alignment axis (orthogonal→uniform) swept and method holds at ≈1.0 throughout; no input-magnitude scaling or breaking point surfaced since the method is exact by construction.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: N/A — purely synthetic/analytic goal with no learned weights to hand-set.

visual_judgement.note: Demo tab pairs true-vs-predicted heatmaps with KL and ablation bar charts; legible, but the huge no_softmax KL can compress the method/uniform comparison on the shared axis.

visualisation_rationale.note: README clearly ties each panel to the claim: heatmaps for cell-by-cell pathway match, KL bars for method-beats-baselines, ablation bars for the causal test.

notes: A solid iteration over first_pass: it reframes attribution as the causal Jacobian ∂O/∂V, adds an own no_softmax strawman, and implements a real top-vs-random key ablation rather than just proposing one. The headline fidelity is near-perfect (≈1.0 across all four regimes), but that is partly because the Jacobian of O=A@V tautologically equals the very softmax(QK^T) attention the generator uses, so the circularity the jury flagged earlier is mitigated in framing more than in substance. Visualisation and baseline comparison are strong; mean of scored items lands in the 'good' band.
